nodes.label: Torah Study
nodes.blurb: The supreme value of learning, laboring in, and never abandoning the Torah.

nodes.label: Chain of Tradition
nodes.blurb: The unbroken handing down of Torah from Sinai through the generations.

nodes.label: Humility
nodes.blurb: Lowliness of spirit and modesty; the refusal to grasp at one's own importance.

nodes.label: Arrogance & Honor-Seeking
nodes.blurb: Pride, the chase after fame and greatness, and their self-destroying power.

nodes.label: Deeds Over Words
nodes.blurb: The primacy of action over speech and study — doing more than one says.

nodes.label: Speech & Silence
nodes.blurb: Guarding one's words, the discipline of silence, and the danger of excess talk.

nodes.label: Fear of Heaven
nodes.blurb: Reverence and awe before God; fear of sin as the root of enduring wisdom.

nodes.label: Reward & Punishment
nodes.blurb: Divine recompense for deeds — and serving without expectation of reward.

nodes.label: Judgment & Reckoning
nodes.blurb: Giving an account before God; the world weighed by the measure of one's deeds.

nodes.label: Justice & the Courts
nodes.blurb: The conduct of judges and witnesses and the fair administration of the law.

nodes.label: Repentance
nodes.blurb: Returning to the right path; teshuvah and good deeds as a shield against ruin.

nodes.label: Friendship & Companions
nodes.blurb: Acquiring a friend, seeking good company, and the influence of those near us.

nodes.label: Teacher & Student
nodes.blurb: Raising disciples, honoring one's teacher, and the bond of learning together.

nodes.label: Peace
nodes.blurb: Loving and pursuing peace; the world held up by the pursuit of harmony.

nodes.label: Truth
nodes.blurb: Truthfulness as one of the pillars on which the world stands.

nodes.label: Kindness & Charity
nodes.blurb: Acts of loving-kindness, generosity, and the differing dispositions of givers.

nodes.label: Love of Others
nodes.blurb: Cherishing people's dignity, judging them favorably, and greeting all warmly.

nodes.label: Anger & Temperament
nodes.blurb: Mastery over anger and the temperaments — being slow to wrath, easy to appease.

nodes.label: Self-Mastery
nodes.blurb: Subduing the evil inclination and ruling one's own spirit and desires.

nodes.label: Envy, Lust & Honor
nodes.blurb: The cravings — envy, lust, and the hunger for honor — that drive a person from the world.

nodes.label: Wealth & Contentment
nodes.blurb: Rejoicing in one's portion; the burdens of property and the meaning of riches.

nodes.label: Work & Livelihood
nodes.blurb: The dignity of labor and the pairing of Torah with a worldly occupation.

nodes.label: Caution Toward Power
nodes.blurb: Wariness of the ruling authorities — even as one prays for the government's welfare.

nodes.label: Communal Responsibility
nodes.blurb: Not separating from the community and leading the many toward merit.

nodes.label: Wisdom
nodes.blurb: Who is truly wise — learning from every person and knowing the marks of the wise.

nodes.label: A Good Name
nodes.blurb: The crown of a good name that surpasses every other crown.

nodes.label: Mortality & This World
nodes.blurb: Life as a vestibule before the world to come; remembering one's end.

nodes.label: Creation & Purpose
nodes.blurb: The world brought forth by divine utterance, fashioned for God's glory.

nodes.label: Care in Mitzvot
nodes.blurb: Treating a light commandment as gravely as a weighty one; one mitzvah drawing another.

nodes.label: Divine Providence
nodes.blurb: All is foreseen yet freedom is granted; everything given against a pledge.

nodes.label: Beloved Israel
nodes.blurb: Humanity made in the divine image and Israel beloved, gifted the Torah.
